title: GLSL vs HLSL — Portability Patterns
description: Systematically maps HLSL concepts to GLSL equivalents (cbuffer → UBO, SV_Position → gl_Position, groupshared → shared), and presents the macro-based portability layer used by engines like id Tech 7 and Frostbite to compile a single shader source to SPIRV and DXIL. Writing portable shaders is a daily requirement for engineers targeting PC, console, and mobile simultaneously. Students will port a provided HLSL PBR shader to GLSL, implement a portability header that abstracts the differences, and verify identical output using RenderDoc frame captures on both backends.
